title: Database Crash at 2AM

slack.message: DB is down, getting connection errors everywhere
Could be DNS, we had a config push earlier tonight
DB logs look clean actually... this is really weird

logs.message: Cache miss storm — falling back to DB for 847 concurrent requests
Connection pool under pressure — 890/900 connections active
Max connections reached — rejecting new connections

red_herrings: DB appears to have crashed but this is caused by the retry storm, not a DB failure
Slack message about DNS config push is a red herring — DNS is fine
correct_root_cause: Cache service connection timeout at 02:00 triggered application-level retry storm. 847 concurrent cache misses fell back to DB simultaneously, exhausting the connection pool from 120 to 900 connections in 3 minutes.

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: Database failure — connection pool exhausted organically
expected_hypothesis_sequence.rejection_evidence: DB logs are completely clean until 02:03. Zero DB errors before the connection spike. DB failures don't start with clean logs — the DB itself is healthy, something external is flooding it.

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: DNS resolution failure caused by the config push
expected_hypothesis_sequence.rejection_evidence: DNS lookups resolving in under 5ms throughout the incident window. The Slack message about a DNS config push is not supported by any metrics data. Cannot reject based on an unverified Slack message.

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: Cache service timeout triggered retry storm that overwhelmed the database
expected_hypothesis_sequence.confirming_evidence: Cache logs show timeout at exactly 02:00 — 3 minutes before DB saturation. API logs show 847 concurrent DB fallbacks at 02:01. DB connection count went from 120 to 890 in 2 minutes, matching the retry storm signature exactly.

action_items: Add circuit breaker to prevent DB fallback storm when cache is unavailable
Set maximum concurrent DB fallback connections to 50% of pool size
Add cache health check to PagerDuty — current alert only monitors DB, not cache
Implement jittered retry backoff to prevent thundering herd on cache recovery
Add SLO alert: cache timeout duration greater than 5s should page immediately
detection_gap: Cache service had no PagerDuty integration. The 02:00 timeout was invisible to on-call until DB saturation triggered the CPU alert at 02:03 — 3 minutes of silent failure with no alerting.
